payload.text:
Cotizador_FuriaRockKids.xlsx
Cotizador
Hoja1
Listas

Cómo usarlo (rápido)
1) Hoja Cotizador

En Referencia eliges el producto.

En Talla seleccionas (se adapta según Adulto/Niño).

Escribes:

Cm_Estampado (tamaño total a estampar)

Cm_Corazon (si aplica el corazón)

Y listo: se calcula Costo_Total → Precio_Venta → Ganancia.

2) Hoja Hoja1 (Base)

Ahí quedó la tabla original, pero ahora con columnas extra para:

Categoria

Cm_Estampado / Cm_Corazon

Costos automáticos y precio final

Para llevarlo a “Google Studio” como página (recomendación práctica)

La forma más fácil de tenerlo como “web app” es:

Opción A (la más rápida): AppSheet + Google Sheets

Sube este Excel a Google Drive y ábrelo como Google Sheets.

Entra a AppSheet → “Create app” → “Start with your data”.

Selecciona la hoja Cotizador como tabla principal.

Configura:

Referencia como “Enum” (dropdown)

Talla como “Enum” (dropdown)

Cm_Estampado y Cm_Corazon como Number

Los campos de costos como “Read-only”

**Defining the Scope**

I'm now focusing on defining the scope for a React-based "Cotizador" app. The goal is a web-based quoter for "Furia Rock Kids", based on the logic described in an Excel file. I'm prioritizing the main calculator sheet's functionality, with dropdowns for products and age (Adult/Kid), along with an input field for the total.


**Refining the Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the feature set. I'm thinking of incorporating input fields for heart-shaped printing areas, calculating *Costo\_Total*, *Precio\_Venta*, and *Ganancia*. I'm also planning to build the React SPA using Tailwind CSS for a modern look, Recharts for visualizations, and potentially integrating the Gemini API for smart product suggestions.


**Detailing the Architecture**

I'm now outlining the component structure, including `Header`, `QuoteForm`, `ResultsCard`, and `PriceBreakdownChart`. I am also planning a `geminiService.ts` for AI-powered sales assistance. I'm starting to define product details like T-shirts, hoodies, and caps, with associated sizes and estimated costs. I have estimated sample values for base costs and print prices.

import React, { useState, useMemo, useEffect } from 'react'; import { QuoteInputs, QuoteResults, Category } from './types'; import { PRODUCT_REFERENCES, TALLAS_NIÑO, TALLAS_ADULTO, COSTO_POR_CM2, COSTO_CM2_CORAZON, MARGEN_DEFAULT } from './constants'; import { getSalesAdvice } from './services/geminiService'; import { PieChart, Pie, Cell, ResponsiveContainer, Tooltip, Legend, BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid } from 'recharts'; import { Calculator, RockingChair as Rock, Tshirt, DollarSign, TrendingUp, Layers, Sparkles, Info, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'; const App: React.FC = () => { const [inputs, setInputs] = useState({ referencia: PRODUCT_REFERENCES[0].id, categoria: 'Niño', talla: TALLAS_NIÑO[3], // Talla 2 cmEstampado: 0, cmCorazon: 0 }); const [aiAdvice, setAiAdvice] = useState(null); const [isAiLoading, setIsAiLoading] = useState(false); // Dynamic calculations const results = useMemo((): QuoteResults => { const product = PRODUCT_REFERENCES.find(p => p.id === inputs.referencia) || PRODUCT_REFERENCES[0]; const costoBase = inputs.categoria === 'Niño' ? product.baseCostNiño : product.baseCostAdulto; const costoEstampado = inputs.cmEstampado * COSTO_POR_CM2; const costoCorazon = inputs.cmCorazon * COSTO_CM2_CORAZON; const costoTotal = costoBase + costoEstampado + costoCorazon; const precioSugerido = Math.ceil((costoTotal * MARGEN_DEFAULT) / 1000) * 1000; // Redondeo al mil más cercano const ganancia = precioSugerido - costoTotal; const margen = (ganancia / precioSugerido) * 100; return { costoBase, costoEstampado, costoCorazon, costoTotal, precioSugerido, ganancia, margen }; }, [inputs]); const handleInputChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent) => { const { name, value } = e.target; setInputs(prev => { const next = { ...prev, [name]: name.includes('cm') ? Number(value) : value }; // Auto-update tallas if category changes if (name === 'categoria') { next.talla = value === 'Niño' ? TALLAS_NIÑO[3] : TALLAS_ADULTO[0]; } // Ensure onesie doesn't allow adult category if (next.referencia === 'onesie-baby') { next.categoria = 'Niño'; } return next; }); }; const generatePitch = async () => { setIsAiLoading(true); const product = PRODUCT_REFERENCES.find(p => p.id === inputs.referencia)!; const advice = await getSalesAdvice(inputs, results, product); setAiAdvice(advice); setIsAiLoading(false); }; const chartData = [ { name: 'Base', value: results.costoBase, color: '#6366f1' }, { name: 'Estampado', value: results.costoEstampado, color: '#ec4899' }, { name: 'Corazón', value: results.costoCorazon, color: '#f59e0b' }, ]; const profitData = [ { name: 'Costo', total: results.costoTotal }, { name: 'Ganancia', total: results.ganancia }, ]; return (
{/* Header */}

FURIA ROCK KIDS

Sistema de Cotización Profesional

Tasa de Margen

{(MARGEN_DEFAULT - 1) * 100}%

{/* Input Section */}

Configurador de Pedido

{/* Referencia */}
{/* Categoría */}
{/* Talla */}
{/* Cm Estampado */}
{/* Cm Corazón */}
{/* Quick Stats */}
Margen Real

{results.margen.toFixed(1)}%

Costos Totales

${results.costoTotal.toLocaleString()}

{/* Results Section */}
{/* Main Price Card */}

Precio de Venta Sugerido

${results.precioSugerido.toLocaleString()} COP
Ganancia: ${results.ganancia.toLocaleString()}

Desglose de Costos

  • Base Prenda: ${results.costoBase.toLocaleString()}
  • Estampado: ${results.costoEstampado.toLocaleString()}
  • Extra/Corazón: ${results.costoCorazon.toLocaleString()}
  • TOTAL: ${results.costoTotal.toLocaleString()}
{/* Decoration */}
{/* Charts */}

Distribución de Costos

{chartData.map((entry, index) => ( ))}

Costo vs Ganancia

{/* AI Sales Assistant */}

Asistente de Ventas IA

Genera argumentos de venta personalizados

{aiAdvice ? (

"{aiAdvice}"

) : (

Pulsa el botón para rockear tu discurso de ventas

)}
{/* Background Glow */}
{/* Footer / Status Bar */}
Sistema Operativo v2.1.0 • Furia Rock Kids
TINTA CM²: ${COSTO_POR_CM2} | CORAZÓN CM²: ${COSTO_CM2_CORAZON}
); }; export default App;

payload.text:
Fix the following errors:

```
Uncaught SyntaxError: The requested module 'lucide-react' does not provide an export named 'Tshirt'
```

**Analyzing the Error**

I'm currently investigating a reported `SyntaxError` related to importing a specific icon, 'Tshirt', from `lucide-react` using an ESM provider. The issue appears to stem from a potential mismatch between the requested export name and the actual library structure, a common pitfall. I'm focusing on verifying the exact export name and checking the import path.

entries.description: Replace non-existent 'Tshirt' icon with 'Shirt' from lucide-react to fix the SyntaxError.

payload.text:
Cálculo correcto (con $170 por cm²)
Costos

Base prenda = precio base (de la referencia)

Estampado principal = cm2_principal * 170

Corazón / extra = cm2_corazon * 170

Planchado = 1000

Empaque = 1300

✅ Costo total

costoTotal

costoTotal=base+(cm2Principal⋅170)+(cm2Corazon⋅170)+1000+1300
2) Ganancia fija de $30.000 (como tú la necesitas)

✅ Precio de venta sugerido

precioVenta
=
costoTotal
+
30000
precioVenta=costoTotal+30000

✅ Ganancia

ganancia
=
30000
ganancia=30000

✅ Tasa de margen real (para mostrar en pantalla)

margen%
=
30000
precioVenta
×
100
margen%=
precioVenta
30000
	​

×100

Ojo: si usas ganancia fija, el “%” va a variar según el costo.

3) Ejemplo con tu pantalla (para validar)

En tu UI: Base $15.000 y Total $17.145 (pero el estampado está mal).

Si fuera correcto con 13 cm² principal:

Estampado = 13 * 170 = $2.210

Si el corazón fuera (por ejemplo) 24 cm²: 24*170 = $4.080

planchado (1000) + empaque (1300)

Ahí el total subiría bastante (como debe ser).

4) Código JS listo (corrige estampado + ganancia fija + evita 60.000000000000001%)

Pégalo en tu función de cálculo (donde actualizas el resumen):

const COSTO_CM2 = 170;
const COSTO_PLANCHADO = 1000;
const COSTO_EMPAQUE = 1300;
const GANANCIA_FIJA = 30000;

function calcularCotizacion({ basePrenda, cm2Principal, cm2Corazon }) {
  const estampado = Number(cm2Principal || 0) * COSTO_CM2;
  const corazon = Number(cm2Corazon || 0) * COSTO_CM2;

  const costoTotal =
    Number(basePrenda || 0) +
    estampado +
    corazon +
    COSTO_PLANCHADO +
    COSTO_EMPAQUE;

  const precioVenta = costoTotal + GANANCIA_FIJA;
  const margen = (GANANCIA_FIJA / precioVenta) * 100;

  return {
    basePrenda: Math.round(basePrenda),
    estampado: Math.round(estampado),
    corazon: Math.round(corazon),
    costoTotal: Math.round(costoTotal),
    ganancia: GANANCIA_FIJA,
    precioVenta: Math.round(precioVenta),
    margenPct: Number(margen.toFixed(2)), // evita 60.000000000000001%
  };
}

5) “No me da la opción de subir la base de datos”

Esto pasa por una de estas 2 razones (te dejo solución directa para ambas):

A) Si es una web app (como tu captura)

Simplemente no tienes el input de archivo. Agrega un botón “Cargar base”:

<input type="file" id="fileBase" accept=".xlsx,.csv" />


Y en JS, lo lees con SheetJS (xlsx) y lo conviertes a JSON para poblar el dropdown.

Si ya estás usando SheetJS, la lógica base es:

document.getElementById("fileBase").addEventListener("change", async (e) => {
  const file = e.target.files[0];
  if (!file) return;

  const data = await file.arrayBuffer();
  const wb = XLSX.read(data, { type: "array" });
  const ws = wb.Sheets[wb.SheetNames[0]];
  const rows = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(ws);

  // rows = tu base de datos (referencia, precio, etc.)
  // aquí llenas el dropdown y guardas en memoria/localStorage
});

B) Si realmente estás en Looker Studio / AppSheet

Ahí no existe “subida de archivo por el usuario” como tal. La base debe estar en:

Google Sheets

BigQuery

CSV publicado como fuente
y el dashboard/app solo “consume” esa fuente.

**Considering Future Calculations**

I'm currently focused on the calculation logic for the "Furia Rock Kids - Cotizador Pro" app. My initial assessment centers on optimizing the existing calculations. The plan is to incorporate the new logic for updates, and later look into importing the database from an Excel or CSV file.


**Updating Calculation Methods**

I've revised the cost and profit calculations in the "Furia Rock Kids - Cotizador Pro" app. Now, the cost per cm² is $170. We're using a fixed profit of $30,000, and the `results` useMemo in App.tsx has been updated. I'm working on adding file input for product data from .xlsx or .csv files, with `SheetJS` (xlsx) to parse the data. I'm also ensuring correct rounding and floating point precision for the margin calculations. Key cost constants were updated in `constants.ts`.

entries.description: Updated QuoteResults to include new fixed costs (planchado and empaque).
Updated pricing constants to match the new $170/cm2 logic and fixed costs.
Updated import map to include SheetJS (xlsx).
Updated calculation logic (fixed profit $30k, $170/cm2) and added Excel database import feature.

payload.text: xml

entries.description: App metadata and configuration.

payload.text:
**Calculating Cotization Details**

I'm focused on the Furia Rock Kids Cotizador app. I've broken down the pricing structure: $170 for the principal and heart, then quantity-based calculations at $1000 per ironing session. There are also fixed prices of $1300 and $300. I need to make sure the app handles this logic correctly.


**Refining Calculation Logic**

I've refined the calculation logic to include the margin and profit. The core formula now includes base garment cost, area-based costs, ironing costs, and fixed costs. The sale price and profit are calculated, and the margin percentage is derived from this. I'm also working on Excel file upload and processing, and I will be using SheetJS for parsing. The reference dropdown will populate from Excel data, and the unit price should automatically update accordingly.

entries.description: Update types to include planchado quantity and detailed cost breakdown for the new calculation logic.
Update constants to match the user's specific pricing: $170/cm2, $1000/planchado, $1300/empaque, and $30000 fixed profit.
Implement dynamic Excel loading, quantity-based ironing calculation, and the fixed $30k profit logic.

payload.text:
**Calculating Quote Details**

I'm currently focused on integrating the pricing formula for the Furia Rock Kids Quote tool. The logic now includes a base cost plus per-square-centimeter charges for both the main print area and the heart/extra print areas: `costoTotal = base + (cm2Principal * 170) + (cm2Corazon * 170)`.


**Refining Price Calculations**

I've added the detailed pricing formula: `precioVenta = costoTotal + 30000`, with defined profit and margin calculations. I've also added the allowance to load an Excel/XLSX file to pre-populate references and base costs. I'm focusing on dynamic dropdowns for categories and sizes now. I also fixed the imports, as well as fixing the Excel parsing logic using the `xlsx` library and am updating the UI to a darker, more rocker-themed design. The `types.ts` and `constants.ts` files are also being updated to reflect refined data structures and fixed costs. Finally, I'm working on specific updates to App.tsx: implement file parsing, implement calculation formulas, category and size selection, and aesthetic integration.

entries.description: Refine types for fixed profit logic and specific product attributes.
Update constants to match user requirements ($170/cm2, $1000/ironing, $30k profit).
Implement dynamic Excel loading, specific $170/cm2 cost logic, and fixed $30k profit calculation.

payload.text:
from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, Table, TableStyle
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.lib import colors
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet, ParagraphStyle

file_path = "/mnt/data/Cotizacion_Cliente.pdf"

doc = SimpleDocTemplate(file_path, pagesize=letter)
styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
title_style = styles['Title']
normal = styles['BodyText']
header = ParagraphStyle('Header', parent=styles['Heading2'], spaceAfter=10)

elements = []

elements.append(Paragraph("COTIZACIÓN", title_style))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 12))
elements.append(Paragraph("Datos del Cliente:", header))
elements.append(Paragraph("Nombre: ____________________________", normal))
elements.append(Paragraph("Fecha: ____________________________", normal))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 18))

elements.append(Paragraph("Detalle de la Cotización:", header))

data = [
    ["Referencia", "Talla", "Cantidad", "Total"],
    ["________________", "____", "____", "$ __________"]
]

table = Table(data, hAlign='LEFT')
table.setStyle(TableStyle([
    ("BACKGROUND", (0,0), (-1,0), colors.black),
    ("TEXTCOLOR", (0,0), (-1,0), colors.white),
    ("ALIGN", (2,1), (-1,-1), "CENTER"),
    ("FONT", (0,0), (-1,0), "Helvetica-Bold"),
    ("BOTTOMPADDING", (0,0), (-1,0), 8),
    ("GRID", (0,0), (-1,-1), 1, colors.grey),
]))

elements.append(table)
elements.append(Spacer(1, 24))

elements.append(Paragraph("Valor Total Cotizado: $ ____________________", styles['Heading2']))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 12))

elements.append(Paragraph("Observaciones:", header))
elements.append(Paragraph("______________________________________________________________", normal))
elements.append(Paragraph("______________________________________________________________", normal))

doc.build(elements)

file_path
Resultado
'/mnt/data/Cotizacion_Cliente.pdf'
Listo, Diego. Te dejé un formato profesional de cotización en PDF, limpio y exactamente con lo que necesitas:

Referencia

Talla

Cantidad

Total

Valor total cotizado

Aquí lo puedes descargar:

👉 Descargar Cotización Cliente – PDF

Si quieres, ahora podemos hacer el paso potente (lo que normalmente se hace en sistemas reales):

✅ Que tu cotizador llene este PDF automáticamente con:

La referencia seleccionada

La talla

La cantidad

El precio final

y que al darle clic a un botón diga:

"Generar cotización"

y descargue el documento listo para enviar por WhatsApp al cliente.

**Re-Evaluate Implementation Strategy**

I'm reconsidering the initial Python-centric approach for generating PDF quotes. The React application's frontend-only architecture poses a challenge. I need to re-evaluate feasible strategies; possibly a serverless function, or client-side PDF generation, depending on complexity and dependencies. Security is also a consideration.


**Refine PDF Generation**

I've decided to proceed with client-side PDF generation using `jsPDF` for its simplicity. I've updated my plan to incorporate `jsPDF` and `jspdf-autotable` into the importmap. I also need to add a "Generar PDF" button to the UI, ideally with a rock-themed design, and implement the PDF generation logic in `App.tsx` to include the quote details. I plan to use a download icon from `lucide-react`. The user flow is clear: data input, button click, local PDF download.


**Integrate PDF Functionality**

I'm now focused on integrating PDF generation directly into the React app. I'll use `jspdf` and `jspdf-autotable` with esm.sh for simplified imports. The PDF will include Reference, Size, Quantity, Total, Valor Total Cotizado, and Observations. I'm adding a button that triggers PDF download. I'm actively working on implementing the table layout and data population within the `App.tsx` file to match the provided requirements.

entries.description: Add jsPDF and jspdf-autotable to the import map for client-side PDF generation.
Integrated jsPDF for generating professional quotes and added a field for observations.

Mostrar siempre los detalles

from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, Table, TableStyle
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.lib import colors
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet, ParagraphStyle

file_path = "/mnt/data/Cotizacion_Cliente.pdf"

doc = SimpleDocTemplate(file_path, pagesize=letter)
styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
title_style = styles['Title']
normal = styles['BodyText']
header = ParagraphStyle('Header', parent=styles['Heading2'], spaceAfter=10)

elements = []

elements.append(Paragraph("COTIZACIÓN", title_style))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 12))

elements.append(Paragraph("Datos del Cliente:", header))
elements.append(Paragraph("Nombre: ____________________________", normal))
elements.append(Paragraph("Fecha: ____________________________", normal))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 18))

elements.append(Paragraph("Detalle de la Cotización:", header))

data = [
    ["Referencia", "Talla", "Cantidad", "Total"],
    ["________________", "____", "____", "$ __________"]
]

table = Table(data, hAlign='LEFT')
table.setStyle(TableStyle([
    ("BACKGROUND", (0,0), (-1,0), colors.black),
    ("TEXTCOLOR", (0,0), (-1,0), colors.white),
    ("FONT", (0,0), (-1,0), "Helvetica-Bold"),
    ("BOTTOMPADDING", (0,0), (-1,0), 8),
    ("GRID", (0,0), (-1,-1), 1, colors.grey),
]))

elements.append(table)
elements.append(Spacer(1, 24))

elements.append(Paragraph("Valor Total Cotizado: $ ____________________", styles['Heading2']))
elements.append(Spacer(1, 12))

elements.append(Paragraph("Observaciones:", header))
elements.append(Paragraph("______________________________________________________________", normal))
elements.append(Paragraph("______________________________________________________________", normal))

doc.build(elements)

entries.description: Fix property access error by using precioUnidad instead of non-existent precioSugerido.
Update call to getSalesAdvice to pass results directly without redundant property augmentation.

payload.text:
**Refining the Application**

I'm now focusing on refining the Furia Rock Kids Cotizador. I've confirmed that the critical fields, `inpPlanchadosQty` and `inpEmpaque`, are present. I'm moving on to implementing the calculation logic, particularly the COST calculation, as it seems central to the core functionality of the application.


**Calculating the Pricing Logic**

I'm now integrating the pricing formula: `Costo Total = Prenda Base + (Cm2 Principal * 170) + (Cm2 Corazón * 170) + (Planchados * 1000) + Empaque`. I'm verifying the variables are available in the code, such as `O_CM2 = 170`, to ensure they align with the current implementation. Next, I'll calculate the `Precio Venta = Costo Total + Ganancia Fija`, incorporating the fixed profit margin. Finally, I'll update the PDF generation to reflect the results of the calculation.


**Validating the Calculations**

I've reviewed the calculation logic in `App.tsx` and confirmed it aligns with the user's intended formulas for calculating costs, including base cost, print costs, and packaging. I'm satisfied that `costoTotalUnidad`, `ganancia`, and `precioUnidad` are being calculated accurately. However, I need to address the user's feedback concerning the visibility and editability of "Empaque" costs. It should be an editable field.


**Implementing Empaque Input**

I'm now incorporating an editable "Empaque" cost field, as requested. I've updated `types.ts` to include `costoEmpaque` in the `QuoteInputs` type and will ensure this value is included in the state of `App.tsx`. I am also working on adding the new input field in the UI with a default value of 1300. Finally, I will ensure the calculation logic correctly uses this input value, for maximum flexibility.


**Clarifying the Profit Structure**

I've examined the profit calculations and confirmed `GANANCIA_FIJA` is added per unit, which aligns with the provided formulas. I'm updating `App.tsx` and `types.ts` to reflect the fixed per-unit profit margin. Also, I will enhance the UI by adding the `costoEmpaque` input field, improving the labeling of the `qtyPlanchado` input, and adding some style flourishes. I'm focusing on ensuring the calculations and the UI are perfectly aligned.

I will update the application to strictly follow your refined calculation logic: incorporating the editable/fixed packaging cost, the specific quantity-based ironing cost, and ensuring the fixed profit of $30,000 is applied correctly per unit. I will also ensure the UI elements have the specific IDs you mentioned for better traceability and that the PDF generation is perfectly aligned with these values.

### Specification:
1.  **State Management**: Added `costoEmpaque` to the inputs state, defaulting to $1,300.
2.  **Calculation Logic**:
    *   **Unit Cost**: `Base + (Cm2 Principal * 170) + (Cm2 Corazon * 170) + (Qty Planchado * 1000) + Costo Empaque`.
    *   **Unit Price**: `Unit Cost + 30,000`.
    *   **Total Quote**: `Unit Price * Total Quantity`.
3.  **UI Updates**:
    *   Added specific input for **Empaque** (Packaging).
    *   Ensured **Planchados** (Ironing) is clearly labeled as quantity.
    *   Applied the requested `id` attributes (`inpPlanchadosQty`, `inpEmpaque`, etc.) to the React components.
4.  **PDF Automation**: The PDF now includes the detailed breakdown of these costs and uses the automated "Generate Quote" flow.

---

entries.description: Add costoEmpaque to QuoteInputs to support the user's specific calculation requirements.
Implement refined calculation logic with editable empaque, quantity-based ironing, and automated PDF generation.

Add garment colors catalog to constants.

payload.text:
**Refining Color Options**

I'm now focused on the color selection feature. I've broken down the requirement to refine the color controls. The core change centers on introducing two distinct selectors, one for "Color Camiseta" (T-shirt Color) and another for "Color Bermuda / Joker" (Bermuda/Jogger Color). I am considering the implications of this change and how to best manage the visibility of these selectors.

entries.description: Update QuoteInputs to support separate colors for T-shirt and Bermuda/Joker.
Implement dual color selectors with conditional visibility for Bermuda/Joker based on product reference name. Update PDF generation to reflect both colors.

payload.text:
**Considering Color Palettes**

I'm currently focused on the color selection UI upgrade. The aim is to move away from dropdowns and toward a button-based "color palette" style for the T-shirt and Bermudas/Joggers selections. This will be more visually intuitive. I have the specific color lists to work with, which is a great start.


**Implementing Color Palettes**

I've just updated `constants.ts` with the color lists for T-shirts and Bermudas. Now, I'm diving into `App.tsx` to implement the new "palette" UI using buttons, mapping over the color arrays. The Bermuda palette will remain conditionally visible based on the "conjunto" state. I need to ensure the existing calculation logic and PDF generation function correctly with the color selections. I'll use Tailwind for styling the grid and buttons, aiming for an active state style with a glow or border.

entries.description: Update color catalogs with the specific T-shirt and Bermuda lists provided by the user.
Implement visually appealing color palettes (button-based selection) for T-shirts and Bermudas. Maintain refined calculation logic and ensure colors appear in PDF.
